title: goodyera venusta
intro: Goodyera venusta, also known as the Rattlesnake Plume or Western Rattlesnake Plantain, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to the western United States and Canada. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating snakebites and other venomous wounds due to its supposed anti-inflammatory and antivenom properties. Research suggests that Goodyera venusta may have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, although more studies are needed to confirm its effectiveness. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are often used to create tinctures, decoctions, and salves, although these should be used with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
